behavioral_changes.summary: Refresh-trigger template data now carries an `mlvExecDefnId` value, and the generated trigger-definition JSON writes that value into the Fabric invocation parameter instead of reusing `triggerId`.
FMLV activator parsing now extracts `fmlvExecutionDefinitionId` from FabricItemInvocation parameter groups and stores it on the action when the extracted value parses as a GUID.
FMLV activator parsing now tolerates malformed trigger-definition structures by returning null or skipping groups when the parameters collection or group arguments are missing.
FMLV activator parsing now ignores extracted execution-definition values that are not valid GUIDs.
The refresh notebook now computes the Fabric API host differently for prod vs. non-prod environments and targets the `/jobs/RefreshMaterializedLakeViews/instances` endpoint.
The refresh notebook now posts a JSON `executionData.mlvExecutionDefinitionId` payload with `Content-Type: application/json` when a nonblank execution-definition id is present.
The refresh notebook now falls back to a header-only POST when the execution-definition id is null, empty, or whitespace after normalization.
The refresh notebook now raises on HTTP failure responses before continuing.
The refresh notebook now guards `Location` header access and only logs the status URL when that header exists.

grounding_evidence.reason: Passes the request execution-definition id into template rendering and defensively normalizes null to an empty string.
Adds the new rendering input so templates can receive an MLV execution-definition id.
Stores the new constructor argument on the template-data object that Scriban will serialize.
Exposes the new field to templates under the snake_case name `mlv_exec_defn_id`.
Changes the generated trigger-definition payload to emit the execution-definition id instead of the trigger id.
Introduces lookup of the new execution-definition id during action parsing.
Adds the valid-GUID branch that populates the action and the defensive guard that rejects invalid values.

grounding_evidence.excerpt:
var parameterGroups = triggerDefinition
    .SelectToken("steps")
    ?.Children<JObject>()
    .SelectMany(s => s.SelectToken("rows")?.Children<JObject>() ?? Enumerable.Empty<JObject>())
    .Where(r => string.Equals((string)r["kind"], "FabricItemInvocation", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase))
    .SelectMany(r => r.SelectToken("arguments")?.Children<JObject>() ?? Enumerable.Empty<JObject>())
    .FirstOrDefault(a => string.Equals((string)a["name"], "parameters", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase))
    ?.SelectToken("values")?.Children<JObject>();
grounding_evidence.reason: Implements the traversal that finds FabricItemInvocation parameter groups containing the execution-definition id.
Short-circuits safely when the trigger definition does not contain a parameters collection.
Skips malformed parameter groups that lack an `arguments` collection instead of dereferencing null.
Defines the exact match-and-read logic for extracting `fmlvExecutionDefinitionId` from a parameter group.
Adds a branch so prod uses the unprefixed Fabric API host while other environments keep the prefixed host.
Changes the notebook to call the new RefreshMaterializedLakeViews instances endpoint.

grounding_evidence.excerpt:
exec_def_id = str(fmlvExecutionDefinitionId).strip() if fmlvExecutionDefinitionId else ''
if exec_def_id:
grounding_evidence.reason: Normalizes the execution-definition id and branches request construction on whether a nonblank value is present.
Adds the JSON content-type header only for the request-body branch.
Builds the new JSON payload shape carrying the execution-definition id.
Uses the new payload-bearing POST when an execution-definition id is available.
Preserves a no-body POST path when there is no usable execution-definition id.
Introduces explicit failure propagation for unsuccessful HTTP responses.
Guards the status-url logic so missing Location headers no longer cause direct indexed access.
Reads the Location header only inside the new presence check.

grounding_evidence.excerpt: print(f"FMLV refresh successfully submitted. Status url: {mlvRefreshStatusUrl}")
grounding_evidence.reason: Limits success logging to responses that actually provide a status URL.

scenario_sketches.title: Render trigger definition with `mlv_exec_defn_id` instead of `trigger_id`
scenario_sketches.rationale: Use distinct `triggerId` and `mlvExecDefnId` values and assert the rendered trigger-definition JSON emits the execution-definition id in the Fabric parameter payload, proving the template no longer reuses `triggerId`.

scenario_sketches.title: Parse a valid `fmlvExecutionDefinitionId` into the action
scenario_sketches.rationale: Build a trigger definition with a FabricItemInvocation `parameters` group whose `parameterName` is `fmlvExecutionDefinitionId` and whose `parameterValue` is a GUID; parsing should populate `Action.FmlvExecutionDefinitionId` with that GUID.

scenario_sketches.title: Gracefully ignore malformed parameter collections while extracting the execution-definition id
scenario_sketches.rationale: Cover missing `steps/rows/parameters.values` and groups missing `arguments`; the helper should return null / skip the group without throwing, exercising both new null-guard paths.

scenario_sketches.title: Reject non-GUID execution-definition values during parsing
scenario_sketches.rationale: Provide a matching `fmlvExecutionDefinitionId` parameter whose value is not a valid GUID; the new `Guid.TryParse` guard should leave `Action.FmlvExecutionDefinitionId` unset.

scenario_sketches.title: Compute correct Fabric host and refresh endpoint across prod and non-prod environments
scenario_sketches.rationale: Validate at least `ENV=prod` and one non-prod prefix to ensure host selection uses the new prod special case and that both paths target `/jobs/RefreshMaterializedLakeViews/instances`.

scenario_sketches.title: Send JSON executionData payload when an execution-definition id is present
scenario_sketches.rationale: With a nonblank `fmlvExecutionDefinitionId`, assert the notebook adds `Content-Type: application/json` and posts a JSON body containing `executionData.mlvExecutionDefinitionId`.

scenario_sketches.title: Skip JSON payload when the execution-definition id is null, empty, or whitespace
scenario_sketches.rationale: Exercise null, empty-string, and whitespace-only inputs; after normalization, the notebook should take the no-body POST branch rather than sending `executionData`.

scenario_sketches.title: Raise immediately on failed refresh submission responses
scenario_sketches.rationale: Mock a 4xx/5xx response and verify the notebook raises via `resp.raise_for_status()` before attempting success-path header access or logging.

scenario_sketches.title: Do not crash when a successful response omits the `Location` header
scenario_sketches.rationale: Mock a successful POST without `Location`; the notebook should skip status-url extraction and success logging instead of throwing a header lookup error.
